arguments.content:
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T13:38:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---

# New Team Member Onboarding Guide

Welcome to the Orion Tech Backend Engineering team! This guide will help you get set up and productive quickly.

---

## Quick Overview

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| **Product** | Project Orion - B2B SaaS project management platform |
| **Team** | Backend Engineering |
| **Tech Lead** | Alex Zhang |
| **Team Members** | Bai, Chen, Dana, Jordan, Sam, Taylor, Casey |
| **Location** | Beijing, China (CST UTC+8) |

---

## Day 1: Account Setup & Access

### 1.1 Required Accounts

Ensure you have access to the following systems. Contact Alex if any are missing:

- [ ] **Company Email** (@oriontech.com)
- [ ] **Slack** - #backend-team, #general, #engineering
- [ ] **GitHub** - Orion Tech organization access
- [ ] **Jira** - Project Orion board
- [ ] **Confluence** - Team documentation
- [ ] **AWS Console** - Staging and development environments
- [ ] **Docker Hub** - Private repository access

### 1.2 Development Machine Setup

#### Prerequisites Checklist

Before starting, ensure your machine has:

| Software | Version | Verification Command |
|----------|---------|---------------------|
| Node.js | 18.x or higher | `node --version` |
| npm | 9.x or higher | `npm --version` |
| Git | 2.35+ | `git --version` |
| Docker | 24.x+ | `docker --version` |
| Docker Compose | 2.20+ | `docker compose version` |
| PostgreSQL Client | 14.x+ | `psql --version` |
| Redis Client | 6.x+ | `redis-cli --version` |
| VS Code | Latest | Check for updates |

#### macOS Setup

```bash
# Install Homebrew if not already installed
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install required tools
brew install node@18 git docker docker-compose postgresql redis

# Install VS Code extensions
code --install-extension esbenp.prettier-vscode
code --install-extension dbaeumer.vscode-eslint
code --install-extension ms-azuretools.vscode-docker
code --install-extension christian-kohler.npm-intellisense
```

#### Linux (Ubuntu) Setup

```bash
# Install Node.js 18
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_18.x | sudo -E bash -
sudo apt-get install -y nodejs

# Install other dependencies
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y git docker.io docker-compose postgresql-client redis-tools

# Add user to docker group
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

---

## Day 2: Repository & Environment Setup

### 2.1 Clone Repositories

```bash
# Create workspace directory
mkdir -p ~/workspace/orion-tech
cd ~/workspace/orion-tech

# Clone the main backend repository
git clone https://github.com/orion-tech/project-orion-backend.git
cd project-orion-backend

# Verify remote access
git remote -v
```

### 2.2 Environment Configuration

```bash
# Copy environment template
cp .env.example .env

# Edit .env with your details
# Required variables to configure:
# - DATABASE_URL (ask Taylor for staging DB credentials)
# - REDIS_URL
# - JWT_SECRET (use a random string for local dev)
# - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
# - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
# - STRIPE_TEST_KEY (for payment testing)

# Open .env in your editor
code .env
```

### 2.3 Install Dependencies

```bash
# Install npm dependencies
npm install

# Verify installation
npm run verify:deps
```

### 2.4 Database Setup

```bash
# Run database migrations
npm run db:migrate

# Seed with development data
npm run db:seed:dev

# Verify database connectivity
npm run db:verify
```

### 2.5 Start Development Environment

```bash
# Option 1: Using Docker Compose (Recommended for new joiners)
docker-compose up -d

# Option 2: Local development (requires local PostgreSQL/Redis)
npm run dev

# Verify services are running
npm run healthcheck
```

The API will be available at `http://localhost:3000`

---

## Day 3: Understanding the Codebase

### 3.1 Project Structure

```
project-orion-backend/
├── src/
│   ├── config/           # Configuration files
│   ├── controllers/      # Route handlers
│   ├── middleware/       # Express middleware
│   ├── models/           # Database models (Sequalize)
│   ├── routes/           # API route definitions
│   ├── services/         # Business logic
│   ├── utils/            # Helper functions
│   └── app.js            # Application entry point
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/             # Unit tests
│   ├── integration/      # Integration tests
│   └── e2e/              # End-to-end tests
├── docs/                 # API documentation
├── scripts/              # Utility scripts
├── migrations/           # Database migrations
└── docker-compose.yml    # Local development stack
```

### 3.2 Key Files to Review

1. `src/app.js` - Application bootstrap
2. `src/routes/index.js` - Route registration
3. `src/config/database.js` - Database configuration
4. `src/middleware/auth.js` - Authentication middleware
5. `.env.example` - Environment variables reference

### 3.3 Running Your First Test

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run specific test file
npm test -- user.controller.test.js

# Run with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run in watch mode (during development)
npm run test:watch
```

---

## Key Code Conventions

### 4.1 Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| Files (JS) | kebab-case | `user-controller.js` |
| Variables | camelCase | `userId`, `isAuthenticated` |
| Constants | UPPER_SNAKE_CASE | `MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS` |
| Classes | PascalCase | `UserService`, `AuthController` |
| Database Tables | snake_case, plural | `users`, `payment_methods` |
| API Endpoints | kebab-case | `/api/v2/user-profiles` |

### 4.2 Code Style

We use **ESLint** and **Prettier** for code consistency:

```bash
# Check code style
npm run lint

# Auto-fix linting issues
npm run lint:fix

# Format with Prettier
npm run format
```

**Key Style Rules:**
- 2 spaces for indentation (no tabs)
- Single quotes for strings
- Semicolons required
- Max line length: 100 characters
- Trailing commas in multi-line objects/arrays

### 4.3 Commit Message Format

Follow conventional commits:

```
type(scope): description

[optional body]

[optional footer]
```

**Types:**
- `feat`: New feature
- `fix`: Bug fix
- `docs`: Documentation only
- `style`: Code style changes (formatting)
- `refactor`: Code refactoring
- `test`: Adding or updating tests
- `chore`: Maintenance tasks

**Examples:**
```bash
feat(auth): add OAuth2 login support
fix(payments): resolve Stripe webhook timeout
docs(api): update v2.3.0 endpoint documentation
test(users): add unit tests for profile update
```

### 4.4 API Development Standards

All API endpoints must:

1. **Include JSDoc comments:**
```javascript
/**
 * Create a new user
 * @route POST /api/v2/users
 * @group Users - User management
 * @param {string} email.body.required - User email
 * @param {string} password.body.required - User password
 * @returns {User.model} 201 - User created
 * @returns {Error} 400 - Invalid input
 * @returns {Error} 409 - Email already exists
 */
```

2. **Validate input using Joi schemas:**
```javascript
const createUserSchema = Joi.object({
  email: Joi.string().email().required(),
  password: Joi.string().min(8).required(),
  name: Joi.string().max(100).optional()
});
```

3. **Return consistent response format:**
```javascript
// Success
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": { "timestamp": "...", "request_id": "..." }
}

// Error
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": { "code": "...", "message": "..." },
  "meta": { "timestamp": "...", "request_id": "..." }
}
```

4. **Include appropriate HTTP status codes**

### 4.5 Testing Requirements

- Minimum 80% code coverage for new code
- Unit tests for all service functions
- Integration tests for API endpoints
- Test both success and failure paths

```javascript
// Example test structure
describe('UserService', () => {
  describe('createUser', () => {
    it('should create a user with valid data', async () => {
      // Arrange
      // Act
      // Assert
    });

    it('should throw error for duplicate email', async () => {
      // Arrange
      // Act
      // Assert
    });
  });
});
```

---

## Commonly Used Commands

### 5.1 Development

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start development server with hot reload |
| `npm start` | Start production server |
| `npm run debug` | Start with debugger attached |
| `npm run healthcheck` | Verify all services are running |

### 5.2 Database

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run db:migrate` | Run pending migrations |
| `npm run db:migrate:undo` | Revert last migration |
| `npm run db:seed` | Run seeders |
| `npm run db:reset` | Reset database (drop + recreate) |
| `npm run db:verify` | Check database connectivity |

### 5.3 Testing

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm test` | Run all tests |
| `npm run test:unit` | Run unit tests only |
| `npm run test:integration` | Run integration tests |
| `npm run test:coverage` | Run tests with coverage report |
| `npm run test:watch` | Run tests in watch mode |

### 5.4 Code Quality

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run lint` | Check code with ESLint |
| `npm run lint:fix` | Auto-fix linting issues |
| `npm run format` | Format code with Prettier |
| `npm run typecheck` | Run TypeScript type checking |

### 5.5 Documentation

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run docs:generate` | Generate API docs from OpenAPI spec |
| `npm run docs:serve` | Serve documentation locally |

### 5.6 Build & Deployment

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run build` | Build for production |
| `npm run docker:build` | Build Docker image |
| `npm run docker:push` | Push image to registry |

---

## Git Workflow

### 6.1 Branch Naming

```
feature/short-description      # New features
bugfix/short-description       # Bug fixes
hotfix/short-description       # Production hotfixes
refactor/short-description     # Code refactoring
docs/short-description         # Documentation updates
```

### 6.2 Daily Workflow

```bash
# Start of day - sync with main
git checkout main
git pull origin main

# Create feature branch
git checkout -b feature/user-profile-api

# Work on your changes...
# ... write code ...
# ... write tests ...

# Commit your work
git add src/controllers/user-controller.js
git commit -m "feat(users): add profile update endpoint"

# Push and create PR
git push -u origin feature/user-profile-api
# Then create Pull Request on GitHub
```

### 6.3 Pull Request Requirements

Before requesting review:

- [ ] All tests passing (`npm test`)
- [ ] Code coverage meets threshold (80%+)
- [ ] Linting passes (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] PR description includes context and testing notes
- [ ] Link to related Jira ticket
- [ ] At least one approver (Alex for backend changes)

---

## Communication Channels

### 7.1 Slack Channels

| Channel | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| #general | Company-wide announcements |
| #backend-team | Backend team discussions |
| #engineering | Cross-team engineering |
| #deployments | Deployment notifications |
| #incidents | Production incident response |

### 7.2 Meeting Cadence

| Meeting | Frequency | Time | Attendees |
|---------|-----------|------|-----------|
| Daily Standup | Daily | 10:00 AM CST | All team members |
| Sprint Planning | Bi-weekly (Mon) | 9:00 AM CST | All team members |
| Sprint Review | Bi-weekly (Fri) | 4:00 PM CST | All + stakeholders |
| Retrospective | Bi-weekly (Fri) | 5:00 PM CST | Engineering only |
| Code Review | Ad-hoc | As needed | PR author + reviewer |

### 7.3 Escalation Path

1. **Technical Questions** → Ask in #backend-team Slack
2. **Code Review** → Tag @alex in PR
3. **Blockers** → Raise in daily standup
4. **Urgent Issues** → Direct message Alex
5. **HR/Admin Questions** → Contact HR team

---

## Tips for Your First Week

1. **Shadow a teammate** - Pair with Bai or Jordan to see the workflow
2. **Ask questions** - No question is too small; we value curiosity
3. **Read documentation** - Check Confluence for architecture docs
4. **Set up IDE** - Install recommended VS Code extensions
5. **Run the full test suite** - Make sure everything works on your machine
6. **Attend standup** - Even if you don't have updates yet
7. **Pick a small ticket** - Look for "good first issue" label in Jira

---

## Resources

### Internal Documentation
- [API Documentation v2.3.0](./20260330_api_v2.3.0_readme.md)
- [Sprint 8 Goals](./20260330_sprint8_week2_goal_statement.md)
- [Coding Standards](https://confluence.orion-tech.com/coding-standards)

### External Resources
- [Node.js Best Practices](https://github.com/goldbergyoni/nodebestpractices)
- [Express.js Documentation](https://expressjs.com/)
- [Sequelize ORM Guide](https://sequelize.org/)
- [Jest Testing Framework](https://jestjs.io/)

---

## Checklist: First Week Completion

### Day 1
- [ ] Access to all accounts granted
- [ ] Development machine prerequisites installed
- [ ] Repository cloned successfully

### Day 2
- [ ] Environment configured (.env file)
- [ ] Dependencies installed
- [ ] Database migrated and seeded
- [ ] Development server running

### Day 3
- [ ] Reviewed project structure
- [ ] Reviewed key codebase files
- [ ] First test run successfully

### Week 1
- [ ] Completed first code review with mentor
- [ ] Pushed first commit
- [ ] Attended first sprint meetings
- [ ] Picked up first Jira ticket
- [ ] One-on-one scheduled with Alex

---

Welcome to the team! We're excited to have you onboard.

**Questions?** Reach out to Alex or post in #backend-team Slack.

*Guide created: March 30, 2026*

content.text:
I've created a comprehensive onboarding guide at `day11/20260330_new_team_member_onboarding_guide.md`.

**Document Structure:**
- **Environment Setup** — Prerequisites checklist, macOS/Linux installation steps, account setup, repository cloning, environment configuration, database setup, and development server startup
- **Code Conventions** — Naming conventions, code style (ESLint/Prettier), commit message format, API development standards, and testing requirements with examples
- **Commonly Used Commands** — Categorized into Development, Database, Testing, Code Quality, Documentation, and Build/Deployment

**Additional sections:**
- Day-by-day onboarding plan (Day 1-3)
- Project structure overview with key files
- Git workflow with branch naming conventions
- Pull request requirements checklist
- Communication channels and meeting cadence
- First week completion checklist
- Links to internal documentation and external resources

The guide provides everything a new team member needs to get productive within their first week.
